'''Hohepa''' "'''Hep'''" '''Cahill''' (born 15 October 1986) is a New Zealand retired professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[NRL]], and [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]] in the [[Super League]]. Cahill also played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Cahill was born in [[Hastings, New Zealand]].

==Playing career==
Cahill went to [[Napier Boys' High School|Napier Boys' College]] in New Zealand, and is also a former age grade New Zealand [[kick boxing]] champion.

Cahill signed with [[Melbourne Storm]] in 2009. He made his [[NRL]] debut in Round 20, 2009.

He played in the 2010 [[World Club Challenge]] against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], being part of the team that lifted the trophy.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-sport/prop-lima-fit-for-storm-clash-20100301-pbdv.html|title=Prop Lima fit for Storm clash|work=The Sydney Morning Herald| date=1 March 2010}}</ref>

On Friday 17 February 2012, Widnes announced they had suspended [[Scott Moore (rugby league)|Scott Moore]], Hep Cahill and [[Simon Finnigan]] for an unspecified breach of club discipline, so missing the match against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on Sunday 19 February 2012.<ref name="Vikings suspend trio">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=7201|title=Vikings suspend trio|publisher=widnesvikings.co.uk|date=2012-02-17|accessdate=2012-02-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120513072629/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=7201|archive-date=13 May 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>
Cahill would play the next eight seasons with Widnes making 168 appearances. Cahill departed the club in 2019 due to salary cap and personal reasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/hep-cahill-to-depart-vikings/|title=Widnes Vikings can confirm that Hep Cahill will leave the club at the end of his current contract, as revealed by the player on his personal Twitter account on Tuesday evening|website=widnesvikings.co.uk}}</ref>
